data.narrative:
CesiumAstro is a Series C-stage Space & Aerospace company that has raised $470M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 5 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Sierra Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, Isar Aerospace, Loft Orbital) and 5 public Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $470M raised proxy, yielding $799M. Peer-median analysis across the 5 same-stage private comparables—which range from $1.0B to $8.0B in valuation—produces a median of $1.1B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because CesiumAstro's revenue is not disclosed, which is standard for private companies, though the broader Space & Aerospace sector trades at a 42.9x median P/S across the 5 public tickers referenced.

These two methods produce a triangulated range of $679M (low) to $1.3B (high), with a median of $974M. The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median reflects uncertainty around CesiumAstro's relative positioning within the sector—specifically, whether its operational maturity and market opportunity align more closely with lower-quartile or median-quartile peers. Key sensitivities include near-term revenue trajectory and capital efficiency relative to the peer cohort.
